expectations.text: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: void Vfosub(const struct sAdamsVforce *vfo, double time, int dflag, int iflag, double *result)

expectations.text: Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: if ( iflag == 5 || iflag == 7 || iflag == 9 ) return;

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_sysary( "TDISP", ipar, 2, disp, 6, &errflg ) to retrieve displacement

expectations.text: Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: spring constant K = vfo->PAR[0], free_len from vfo->PAR[1], markers from vfo->I and vfo->JFLOAT

expectations.text: Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
expectations.evidence: response.md: mdi.bat handles adams_util.lib automatically via cr-u flag — no explicit lib name needed

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: response.md: mdi.bat cr-u n vfosub.c -n my_sub.dll ex

expectations.text: Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
expectations.evidence: response.md: "I can run the build now using the agent workflow:" followed by generate_adams_env.py and call + mdi.bat steps

expectations.text: Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow
expectations.evidence: response.md: "> Fallback (only if agent compilation fails): Open Start Menu → Adams <version> → Command Prompt"
